## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/slides/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → FigJam file, use get_figjam; pass the original board URL as figjamUrl when available
- figma.com/slides/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → Figma Slides file

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- diff-review: Explain an implementation diff and produce a prioritized code review checklist. Use when the user asks for /diff-review, local code review, diff explanation, or what reviewers should inspect first.
- review: Multi-persona code review — Linus, Fowler, and Vogels each review a PR and have a discussion round. Uses local Claude token. Run as /review <PR_NUMBER>.
- fastapi: FastAPI best practices — project structure, route patterns, Pydantic v2, dependency injection, settings, error handling. References: fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template, benavlabs/FastAPI-boilerplate, zhanymkanov/fastapi-best-practices, fastapi-practices/fastapi_best_architecture.
- changelog-generator: Automatically creates user-facing changelogs from git commits by analyzing commit history, categorizing changes, and transforming technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Turns hours of manual changelog writing into minutes of automated generation.
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
- feature: Interactive feature workflow — gather requirements interactively (description, API list, branch name, context), then run design → implement → PR in sequence. Use when the user wants to build a new feature end-to-end.
- plan-for-codex: Analyze the user's request and write a PLAN.md at the worktree root that Codex (running in another tab on the same worktree) can read and implement. Do not modify any code yourself — only produce the plan. Use when the user wants Claude to design and Codex to implement.
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- assignment: End-to-end assignment workflow — loads tech stack constraints → /think (design, 7 stages) → /iterate (Codex implements) → README polish → final gate. Usage: /assignment <assignment description or path>
- diff-review: Diff Review Brief
- iterate: Multi-agent iteration loop with human checkpoints — Pre-build → Builder → Spot-check → Tester → Reviewer → Human. Usage: /iterate <feature description>
- plan: Design Agent — 3-phase collaborative design with human checkpoints at each stage. Outputs REQUIREMENTS.md + PLAN.md. Usage: /plan
- design: Analyze requirements and create a design spec before implementation. Writes to /tmp/spec.md and waits for user approval.
- think: Design thinking partner — walks alongside you through 7-stage expert planning. Shows the internal monologue of a senior engineer at each stage, then asks sharp questions to sharpen your thinking. Usage: /think <problem or decision>
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Find and apply agent rules from anthropics assignment

input.prompt:
Search the Anthropic GitHub organization for useful agent patterns, rules, and best practices that could be applied to a FastAPI assignment workflow.

Specifically look at:
1. https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-cookbook - any agentic patterns, multi-agent examples
2. https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code - CLAUDE.md patterns, rules files, hooks
3. https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python - any relevant patterns
4. Look for any other Anthropic repos with agent orchestration patterns, rules/guardrails, or CLAUDE.md examples

I'm interested in:
- Multi-agent orchestration patterns (subagents, parallel agents)
- Rules files (like ~/.claude/rules/*.md) that enforce coding standards
- CLAUDE.md patterns for assignment/take-home projects
- Tool use patterns for LLM calls
- Any guardrail patterns (deterministic regex checks, etc.)
- Hooks or automation patterns for CI-style workflows

For each useful thing you find, give:
- The repo and file path
- What it does
- How it could be applied to a FastAPI + Anthropic SDK assignment workflow

Use WebFetch to actually read the content of promising files. Report findings in under 400 words.

content.text:
Here are the key findings across Anthropic repos, with direct applicability to your FastAPI + Anthropic SDK assignment workflow:

---

## 1. `anthropic-cookbook` — `patterns/agents/` (orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer)

**Repo path:** `anthropics/anthropic-cookbook/patterns/agents/`
Three patterns: `basic_workflows.ipynb`, `orchestrator_workers.ipynb`, `evaluator_optimizer.ipynb` — the reference implementation for [Building Effective Agents](https://anthropic.com/research/building-effective-agents).

**What it does:** Prompt chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator→subagent delegation, and evaluator→optimizer revision loops.

**Apply to your workflow:** Your `/assignment` workflow already runs design → implement → gate in sequence. The evaluator-optimizer pattern fits directly into your gate stage — have a second agent score the implementation against the spec before passing. The orchestrator-workers pattern maps to how your `llm.py` could dispatch parallel subagent calls (e.g., run guardrails analysis and code review concurrently).

---

## 2. `anthropic-cookbook` — `managed_agents/CMA_*.ipynb`

**Repo path:** `anthropics/anthropic-cookbook/managed_agents/`
Ten notebooks covering human-in-the-loop (`CMA_gate_human_in_the_loop.ipynb`), test-fix loops (`CMA_iterate_fix_failing_tests.ipynb`), multi-specialist teams (`CMA_coordinate_specialist_team.ipynb`), and outcome grading (`CMA_verify_with_outcome_grader.ipynb`).

**What it does:** The gate notebook shows the `requires_action` idle bounce + custom `decide()`/`escalate()` tool pattern — deterministic human approval before any mutation. The coordinate notebook shows per-role tool scoping (`multiagent` coordinator config with scoped toolsets per specialist).

**Apply to your workflow:** The `requires_action` pattern is directly applicable to your service layer's guardrail flow — block at `service/` before hitting `crud/`, using a custom tool as the decision point rather than free-text response. Per-role tool scoping maps to your layer separation rules: each "agent" role only gets tools appropriate to its layer.

---

## 3. `claude-code` — `plugins/security-guidance/hooks/security_reminder_hook.py`

**Repo path:** `anthropics/claude-code/plugins/security-guidance/hooks/security_reminder_hook.py`

**What it does:** PreToolUse hook — regex-scans file edits for dangerous patterns (`os.system()`, `eval()`, `pickle`, `child_process.exec`, `innerHTML`), blocks with exit code 2, shows a warning with the safe alternative once per session. Session-scoped deduplication avoids noise.

**Apply to your workflow:** This is structurally identical to what your `guardrails.py` does (deterministic regex R1–R5), but as a hook rather than inline service code. You could add a `.claude/hooks/` pretooluse hook that enforces the same rules as your guardrails before any Edit/Write to `routes.py` — e.g., block any route that directly imports `crud/` or calls `HTTPException` inside `service/`.

---

## 4. `claude-code` — `plugins/hookify/hooks/pretooluse.py`

**Repo path:** `anthropics/claude-code/plugins/hookify/hooks/pretooluse.py`

**What it does:** Rule-engine hook — reads `.claude/hookify.*.local.md` files and evaluates them against the attempted bash or file operation. Exits 0 (non-blocking) with feedback, gracefully degrades on import failures.

**Apply to your workflow:** The `.local.md` rule file pattern is worth adopting. You could write `hookify.fastapi.local.md` rules like "if Edit targets `routes.py`, check that no SQLAlchemy imports are added" — enforcing layer separation at edit-time rather than at test time only.

---

## 5. `anthropic-cookbook` — `.claude/commands/review-pr.md`

**Repo path:** `anthropics/anthropic-cookbook/.claude/commands/review-pr.md`

**What it does:** A slash command that uses `Task` tool with `subagent_type: "code-reviewer"` to delegate the actual diff analysis to a specialist subagent, then structures output with checkboxes and file:line references.

**Apply to your workflow:** The `Task` + `subagent_type` pattern is the correct way to spawn a bounded reviewer subagent from your `/assignment` gate stage. Your current gate is implicit — making it an explicit `.claude/commands/gate.md` that calls `Task(subagent_type: "code-reviewer")` with the REQUIREMENTS.md + diff as context gives you a repeatable, auditable gate.

---

## 6. `anthropic-cookbook` — `CLAUDE.md` + `.claude/skills/cookbook-audit/`

**Repo path:** `anthropics/anthropic-cookbook/CLAUDE.md` and `.claude/skills/cookbook-audit/SKILL.md`

**Key patterns:**
- CLAUDE.md lists slash commands available in both Claude Code and CI (`/notebook-review`, `/model-check`, `/link-review`) — making the dev workflow and CI workflow identical
- `cookbook-audit` skill runs `python3 validate_notebook.py` as step 3 before any LLM review — deterministic checks first, LLM second
- `validate_notebook.py` integrates `detect-secrets` with a baseline file, custom plugin patterns, and checks against `.secrets.baseline`

**Apply to your workflow:** The "deterministic first, LLM second" ordering mirrors your `guardrails.py` design principle. You could add a `validate_submission.py` that runs ruff + pytest + guardrail smoke tests before any LLM gate review fires. The `.secrets.baseline` pattern is directly applicable to prevent committing `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in test fixtures.

---

**Summary of the most actionable patterns:**
- Evaluator-optimizer loop for the gate stage (cookbook `patterns/agents/evaluator_optimizer.ipynb`)
- `requires_action` idle bounce for human-in-the-loop approval (managed_agents gate notebook)
- PreToolUse hook for enforcing layer separation at edit-time (security hook + hookify)
- `Task(subagent_type: "code-reviewer")` for bounded specialist delegation in slash commands
- Deterministic validation script before LLM review (cookbook-audit skill pattern)

---
name: assignment
description: End-to-end assignment workflow — loads tech stack constraints → /think (design, 7 stages) → /iterate (Codex implements) → README polish → final gate. Usage: /assignment <assignment description or path>
---

# Assignment Workflow

Feature/Task: **$ARGUMENTS**

Four phases in sequence. Do not skip or reorder.

---

## Phase 0 — Load Stack Constraints

Before any design begins, detect the tech stack and load the relevant skill as active constraints.

**Detection**: read the assignment spec (if a file path is given) or infer from $ARGUMENTS.

If the project uses **FastAPI / Python backend**:
→ Read `~/.claude/commands/fastapi.md` in full.
→ All patterns in that file are now **mandatory constraints** for Phase 1 design and Phase 2 implementation.
→ Tell the user:

```
Stack detected: FastAPI + Python
Loaded constraints from /fastapi skill:
  ✓ 3-tier schema (Input / Out / Domain)
  ✓ api/ → service/ → crud/ layer separation
  ✓ Annotated dependency aliases in deps.py
  ✓ Pydantic v2 (ConfigDict, model_dump, model_validate)
  ✓ lifespan instead of on_event
  ✓ Domain exceptions, not HTTPException in service layer

이 제약들이 Phase 1 설계와 Phase 2 구현에 적용돼.
```

If other stacks are detected (Next.js, Go, etc.) — note them and proceed without FastAPI constraints.

---

## Phase 1 — Think (Design)

Run the full `/think` flow for: $ARGUMENTS

Walk through all 8 stages (Stage 0 → Stage 7):
- Stage 0: 첫 궁금증 (5 first curiosity questions)
- Stage 1: 문제 이해
- Stage 2: AI 탐색 설계
- Stage 3: 결과 검증
- Stage 4: Trade-off 판단
- Stage 5: Edge Case 발견
- Stage 6: 방향 수정
- Stage 7: 최종 결정

At each stage: narrate the expert inner monologue → ask sharp questions → wait for user response → validate and move on.

**FastAPI-specific probes** (add these at the relevant stages if stack is FastAPI):

- Stage 0: "이 엔드포인트가 `service/`를 거쳐야 해, 아니면 `crud/`를 직접 불러도 돼?"
- Stage 1: "응답 schema가 몇 tier 필요해? Input / Out만? 아니면 DB domain model도?"
- Stage 4: "route → service → crud 레이어를 지키면 뭘 포기하게 돼? 그게 합리적이야?"
- Stage 5: "DB 세션 커밋/롤백이 어느 레이어 책임이야? `crud`가 commit하면 안 되는 이유는?"

At Stage 7, after the user writes the final decision sentence, write `/tmp/spec.md`:

```markdown
# Implementation Spec — [feature name]

## Problem
[from Stage 0: who uses it, failure cost, scope boundary]

## Decision
[Stage 7 final decision sentence]

## Why
[Stage 4 trade-off rationale]

## Edge Cases to Handle
[Stage 5 findings]

## What NOT to build
[Stage 0 out-of-scope items]

## Implementation Constraints
[all constraints from stages 1–6]

### Code Style (FastAPI — mandatory if stack detected in Phase 0)
- Project structure: api/ → service/ → crud/ (routes never call crud directly)
- Schemas: 3 tiers — {Feature}Create (input), {Feature}Out (response), SQLAlchemy model (DB)
- Dependencies: Annotated aliases in api/deps.py — DBSession, CurrentUser
- Pydantic v2: ConfigDict(from_attributes=True), model_dump(), model_validate()
- App factory: lifespan context manager, not @app.on_event
- Errors: domain exceptions in service/, handler registered in main.py
- No HTTPException inside service layer
- response_model= explicit when return type differs from serialized type

## Files to touch
[expected file list following fastapi structure:
 api/v1/endpoints/, service/, crud/, models/, schemas/, core/config.py]
```

Then show:

```
Phase 1 complete. 설계 확정.

다음: Phase 2에서 Codex가 구현을 시작해.
계속할까? (yes / 설계 수정)
```

Wait for confirmation before Phase 2.

---

## Phase 2 — Iterate (Implementation)

Run `/iterate` for each feature/task identified in Phase 1.

If spec covers a single feature:
```bash
{ cat /tmp/spec.md; echo -e "\nAfter implementation, write a summary of all changes to /tmp/reply.txt"; } | codex exec --full-auto
cat /tmp/reply.txt
```

If spec covers multiple features, run `/iterate <feature>` for each one in priority order, showing the user the result between each and asking to continue.

After all iterations complete, proceed to Phase 3.

---

## Phase 3 — README Polish

Read the current `README.md` and compare against the actual code. Check each of these:

1. **Architecture diagram vs routes** — every URL in the diagram must match actual route decorators:
   ```bash
   grep -rn "@router\." app/api/
   ```

2. **False claims** — any `→ validate X` or `→ check Y` in the diagram that isn't in the code must be removed.

3. **Response shape accuracy** — every `→ returns: {...}` must match the actual `response_model` fields.

4. **Trade-offs table** — every significant design decision from Stage 4 must have a row.

5. **AI Leverage table** — every file touched in Phase 2 must be listed with verification method.

6. **§If More Time** — anything ruled out in Stage 0 must appear here as a named P2 item.

Fix any gaps found, then proceed to Phase 4.

---

## Phase 4 — Final Gate (must all pass before commit)

Run these and show each result:

```bash
uv run pytest -v
uv run ruff check app/
```

| Check | Command | Must pass |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| Tests green | `uv run pytest -v` | 0 failed |
| Lint clean | `uv run ruff check app/` | no output |
| Layer separation | `grep -rn "from app.crud" app/api/` | no direct crud import in routes |
| URLs match README | `grep -rn "@router\." app/api/` | matches architecture diagram |
| No false claims | read README §Architecture | every claim exists in code |
| Response shapes | read README §Architecture | matches response_model fields |
| Trade-offs complete | read README §4 | every Stage 4 decision documented |
| AI Leverage complete | read README §3 | every Phase 2 file listed |

Show result as:

```
Phase 4 — Final Gate

✓ pytest: N passed
✓ ruff: clean
✓ Layer separation: routes don't import crud directly
✓ README URLs consistent
✓ No false claims
✓ Response shapes accurate
✓ Trade-offs documented
✓ AI Leverage complete

Ready to commit. 커밋 메시지 확인 후 push.
```

If any check fails: fix it before showing this summary. Do not mark ✓ for a check you haven't run.

1	# Assignment Execution Protocol
2	# Agentic IDE IC1 Backend Engineer — Take-Home Playbook
3	
4	> **How to use**: Give me this file + the assignment file at the start of a session.
5	> I will execute the phases below in order, using worktrees and parallel agents where specified.
6	> Target: 90%+ pass-rate output within the stated time budget.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## JD Signal Map — Read Before Any Code
11	
12	Every implementation choice below maps to a JD keyword. If a choice is not on this map, it is P2.
13	
14	| JD Keyword | What to show in code | Where it lands |
15	|---|---|---|
16	| `Git worktrees` | Two worktrees, parallel branches, explicit merge step | Phase 3 setup, commit history |
17	| `multi-agent` | Agent A (routes) + Agent B (guardrails) on separate worktrees | Phase 3 prompt framing |
18	| `AGENTS.md / Engineering Manifesto` | File loaded just-in-time in every LLM call; brand-specific override path described | `src/llm.py`, README §Architecture |
19	| `guardrails — safe to deploy` | `GuardrailCheck(ruleId, severity, result, reason)` — BLOCK stops merge | `src/guardrails.py`, README §Domain Model |
20	| `multi-brand (efood/glovo/talabat)` | `brand` field on Session; guardrail loader parametrized by brand | Schema, guardrail signature |
21	| `context integration` | AGENTS.md injected as system-prompt context, not hardcoded strings | `src/llm.py` system prompt |
22	| `OTEL / OAM / tracing` | `trace_id` on Session; P2 note for OAM export | Schema + README §If More Time |
23	| `deterministic boundary` | Regex-first guardrails; LLM only for plan/patch generation | README §Architecture diagram |
24	| `developer productivity / friction` | README §Problem opens with the manual workflow this replaces | README §1 Problem |
25	| `customizing cutting-edge agentic IDE` | "This is not a Cursor clone — it is the DH-aware integration layer above Cursor" | README §1 first line |
26	| `measurement / KPI` | `GuardrailCheck` output feeds PR/engineer and Human/AI code ratio dashboards | README §If More Time |
27	
28	**Mandatory before submitting**: every row above must map to ≥1 line of code or README text.
29	
30	---
31	
32	## Design Principles — Apply Before Writing Any Schema or Route
33	
34	These five principles gate every implementation decision. If a choice conflicts with one, stop and reconsider.
35	
36	### 1. SRP — Single Responsibility per Endpoint
37	
38	Each endpoint = one state transition = one responsibility.
39	
40	| Endpoint | Responsibility | What it must NOT do |
41	|---|---|---|
42	| `POST /sessions` | Create session entity | Generate plan, touch LLM |
43	| `POST /sessions/{id}/plan` | Decompose description → `[PlanStep]` with `patches: []` | Generate diffs, run guardrails |
44	| `POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches` | Generate diff for one PlanStep → `PatchProposal` with `checks: []` | Run guardrails, modify other steps |
45	| `POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check` | Run guardrails → `[GuardrailCheck]` | Generate new diffs, re-plan |
46	| `GET /sessions/{id}` | Return accumulated state | Trigger any computation |
47	
48	**Smell check**: if a route calls both `generate()` and `check_patch()`, it violates SRP.
49	
50	**Schema SRP corollary**: domain models (storage/response) and LLM input schemas are different things.
51	- Domain model: `PlanStep(id, description, target_files, patches=[])` — full state with children
52	- LLM input schema: `PlanStepInput(description, target_files)` — only what the LLM generates
53	- Never pass a domain model schema to the LLM if it contains nested children (id, created_at, child lists).
54	  The LLM will fill them in, breaking the SRP of every downstream endpoint.
55	
56	---
57	
58	### 2. Deterministic Boundary
59	
60	The LLM is always sandwiched between deterministic code. Non-determinism is quarantined.
61	
62	```
63	[deterministic in]  →  [LLM]  →  [deterministic out]
64	  schema validation     proposes    schema validation
65	  AGENTS.md load        only        guardrail regex
66	  brand resolution               Pydantic parsing
67	```
68	
69	Rules:
70	- `src/guardrails.py` contains **zero** LLM calls. Regex only.
71	- `src/llm.py` contains **zero** business logic. It is a transport layer.
72	- LLM output is always parsed through Pydantic before it touches application state.
73	- Use `tool_use` + `tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}` — never free-text JSON parsing.
74	
75	---
76	
77	### 3. YAGNI — You Aren't Gonna Need It
78	
79	Build exactly what the spec asks. Nothing more.
80	
81	| Temptation | Decision |
82	|---|---|
83	| SQLite / Postgres | In-memory dict. Persistence is P2 — document the swap path. |
84	| Auth / API keys | Not in spec. Padding scope is a red flag for evaluators. |
85	| LLM-as-judge for guardrails | Regex covers R1–R5 exactly. LLM-as-judge is P2. |
86	| Retry loop / Evaluator-Optimizer | P2. Document it in README §If More Time. |
87	| Multi-brand AGENTS.md routing | `brand` parameter in signature. Second brand file is P2. |
88	| Caching | Not needed within a 90-min session lifetime. |
89	
90	If it is not in the spec and not a JD signal, it is P2 — mention in README, do not implement.
91	
92	**YAGNI applies to response shape too — Response Schema = Workflow Signal.**
93	
94	The shape of a response communicates what the endpoint did. Empty child collections in a response are not "free" — they send a false signal.
95	
96	```
97	# Wrong: POST /plan returns this
98	{"id": "...", "description": "...", "target_files": [...], "patches": []}
99	#                                                           ^^^^^^^^^^
100	#                                              implies patches are part of planning
101	
102	# Right: POST /plan returns this
103	{"id": "...", "description": "...", "target_files": [...]}
104	# shape says: "I produced a plan step. patches are someone else's job."
105	```
106	
107	Rule: **each endpoint's response schema contains only the fields that endpoint created.**
108	Use a dedicated response model (e.g. `PlanStepOut`, `PatchProposalOut`) without child lists — do not reuse the full domain model as the response type.
109	
110	```python
111	# models.py — response schemas (one per endpoint that creates a new entity)
112	class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):
113	    id: UUID
114	    description: str
115	    target_files: list[str]
116	    # no patches — POST /plan does not create patches
117	
118	class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):
119	    id: UUID
120	    planStepId: UUID
121	    diff: str
122	    # no checks — POST /patches does not run guardrails
123	```
124	
125	The full domain model (`PlanStep` with `patches`, `PatchProposal` with `checks`) is only used in `GET /sessions/{id}` — the accumulated state view.
126	
127	---
128	
129	### 4. Workflow-first Design
130	
131	Design the workflow (sequence of state transitions) before designing schemas or routes.
132	
133	**Step 1 — Draw the workflow as a state machine first:**
134	```
135	∅ → Session(steps=[]) → Session(steps=[PlanStep(patches=[])]) → Session(steps=[PlanStep(patches=[PatchProposal(checks=[])])]) → Session(steps=[...(checks=[GuardrailCheck...])])
136	```
137	
138	**Step 2 — Each arrow = one endpoint.**
139	Each endpoint advances state by exactly one step. No endpoint skips a step or does two steps at once.
140	
141	**Step 3 — Schema flows from workflow, not from database convenience.**
142	Ask: "what is the minimal input the LLM needs to do its job?" → that is the LLM input schema.
143	Ask: "what is the full state a reviewing engineer needs?" → that is the domain model.
144	
145	---
146	
147	### 5. Explainability
148	
149	Every AI decision must be traceable by a human reviewer.
150	
151	| Layer | Explainability mechanism |
152	|---|---|
153	| Plan generation | `PlanStep.description` is human-readable; `target_files` is explicit |
154	| Patch generation | `PatchProposal.diff` is a standard unified diff — reviewable in any diff tool |
155	| Guardrail result | `GuardrailCheck.ruleId` + `reason` — cites the specific AGENTS.md rule |
156	| Session audit | `Session.trace_id` — future OTEL/OAM hook |
157	| AI vs human work | README §AI Leverage table — explicit, honest accounting |
158	
159	**Rule**: No GuardrailCheck result without a `reason` that names the specific rule (`per AGENTS.md R4`).
160	A `result: "fail"` with `reason: "error"` is worse than useless — it is unactionable.
161	
162	---
163	
164	## Meta-Principles (from Anthropic engineering blog + agent-architecture.md)
165	
166	- **Deterministic sandwich**: Every LLM call sits between deterministic input validation and deterministic output verification. The LLM proposes; the code decides.
167	- **Workflow backbone + one agent loop**: LLM loop only where genuinely non-deterministic. Guardrails are always deterministic.
168	- **Just-in-time context**: Load AGENTS.md at call time — never pre-dump into module-level startup.
169	- **Single agent > multi-agent for the coding loop**: Use parallel worktrees for *independent implementation tracks*, not for agents collaborating on the same task.
170	- **README is the deliverable**: Code is proof it works. The README demonstrates you think like a system designer.
171	- **DH framing**: This service is a "DH-aware integration layer" — it augments Cursor/Claude Code with brand context and guardrails. It is not a replacement IDE.
172	
173	## Slash Command Contract — `/assignment`
174	
175	The project command lives in both `.claude/commands/assignment.md` and
176	`case-01-session-backend/.claude/commands/assignment.md`. Keep them in sync.
177	
178	Pattern borrowed from Anthropic's `anthropics/claude-code` project commands:
179	
180	- Use command frontmatter (`description`, `allowed-tools`) so the command advertises its purpose and narrows tool use.
181	- Inject live context at invocation time (`git status`, assignment text, protocol, AGENTS.md) instead of relying on stale memory.
182	- Treat `$ARGUMENTS` as extra user input, not as a replacement for the assignment or protocol.
183	- Start with a todo list and update it as phases complete.
184	- Never invent requirements. If the assignment is ambiguous, document the assumption rather than expanding scope.
185	- Be conservative with automation: do not push, open PRs, or post external comments unless the user explicitly asks.
186	- End every `/assignment` run with files changed, verification run, and remaining checks.
187	
188	If a command behavior conflicts with this protocol, the protocol wins.
189	
190	---
191	
192	## Phase 0 — Decompose (0:00 – 0:05)
193	
194	**Who runs this**: Me (Claude Code), immediately on receiving the assignment.
195	
196	### 0.1 XY-30S Decomposition (4 bullets, 30 seconds)
197	
198	```
199	- WHO uses this?           → developer requesting an AI-assisted code change
200	- WHAT friction removed?   → manual diff review + rule compliance check against AGENTS.md
201	- NON-DETERMINISTIC parts? → plan decomposition, patch generation (LLM); rule checking (regex)
202	- EXTERNAL integrations?   → Anthropic API, AGENTS.md file, brand config
203	```
204	
205	### 0.2 Risk Inventory
206	
207	| Risk | Mitigation |
208	|------|-----------|
209	| LLM latency in tests | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` missing → mock mode; real call behind flag |
210	| Storage underspecified | In-memory dict; documented swap path to Postgres |
211	| Rule ambiguity in AGENTS.md | Regex-first; LLM-as-judge only if regex is insufficient |
212	| Multi-brand complexity | `brand` field drives AGENTS.md path; single override dict per brand |
213	| Time overrun | P0/P1/P2 locked before first line of code |
214	
215	### 0.3 P0 / P1 / P2 Cut List
216	
217	| Tier | Scope | Time |
218	|------|-------|------|
219	| **P0** | All 5 endpoints + 2 deterministic guardrails (R4, R5 minimum) + 1 pytest green | 0–75 min |
220	| **P1** | Real Anthropic SDK call with structured JSON output; `trace_id` on every response; brand-parametrized guardrail loader | 75–85 min |
221	| **P2** | OTEL export to OAM; SQLite persistence; multi-brand AGENTS.md override; auth; SKILL.md Skills pattern | README mention only |
222	
223	---
224	
225	## Phase 1 — README Skeleton + Domain Model (0:05 – 0:20)
226	
227	### 1.1 README.md — Write the 5 sections in English, all blanks filled with placeholders
228	
229	```markdown
230	# Session Backend — Agentic IDE Integration Layer
231	
232	> This service is the DH-aware integration layer that sits above Cursor/Claude Code:
233	> it injects brand context (AGENTS.md), proposes code patches via LLM, and enforces
234	> guardrails before any generated diff can be merged.
235	> The LLM proposes; deterministic checks decide.
236	
237	## 1. Problem & Approach
238	
239	**What this replaces**: a developer manually reviewing LLM-generated diffs against
240	their brand's Engineering Manifesto (AGENTS.md) before merging.
241	
242	**Architecture**: deterministic sandwich —
243	- deterministic input (AGENTS.md loaded just-in-time, brand resolved from session)
244	- LLM call (plan decomposition or patch generation, schema-constrained output)
245	- deterministic output (guardrail regex checks, severity-gated merge decision)
246	
247	**Assumptions** (5+):
248	1. In-memory storage is sufficient for a session-scoped prototype; persistence is P2.
249	2. AGENTS.md lives at the repo root and is the authoritative rule source.
250	3. Severity BLOCK = merge blocked; WARN = visible to developer but not blocking.
251	4. LLM output is always validated against the Pydantic schema before storage.
252	5. Each brand (efood/glovo/talabat) can have its own AGENTS.md path; currently efood only.
253	6. `trace_id` is generated per-session for future OTEL export.
254	
255	**Ambiguities I noticed**:
256	1. Should WARN rules accumulate across patches and block after N warnings?
257	2. Is the LLM allowed to propose changes to AGENTS.md itself?
258	3. Who owns rule addition — platform team or brand team?
259	
260	## 2. Domain Model
261	
262	- `Session` — top-level entity; carries `brand`, `trace_id`, and all child lists
263	- `PlanStep` — one proposed change (description + target_files)
264	- `PatchProposal` — unified diff string for one PlanStep
265	- `GuardrailCheck` — one rule result: `ruleId`, `severity`, `result`, `reason`
266	
267	```
268	Session 1—* PlanStep 1—* PatchProposal 1—* GuardrailCheck
269	```
270	
271	Severity ladder: `BLOCK` (security/brand violation) > `WARN` (style) > `INFO` (suggestion).
272	A patch with any `BLOCK` check cannot be merged. This feeds the Human/AI code ratio KPI
273	by flagging which AI-generated diffs required human intervention.
274	
275	## 3. AI Leverage
276	
277	| Part | Done by | Verification |
278	|------|---------|--------------|
279	| Domain models | Hand | Type-checked by Pydantic + mypy |
280	| FastAPI routes | AI (Claude Code, worktree feat/routes) | Hand-reviewed diff |
281	| Guardrail regex rules | AI (Claude Code, worktree feat/guardrails) | Pytest: R2/R4/R5 BLOCK cases |
282	| LLM prompt design | Hand | Manual smoke test with real API key |
283	| README | Hand | — |
284	
285	Every AI-generated file went through: `ruff check` → `pytest` → manual diff scan.
286	No AI output was committed without a test covering the specific behavior.
287	
288	"The LLM only performs extraction and proposal generation.
289	Merging, validation, and guardrail evaluation are deterministic."
290	
291	## 4. Trade-offs & Decisions
292	
293	| Decision | Rationale | Reconsider if |
294	|----------|-----------|---------------|
295	| In-memory storage | Removes DB setup from the critical path; session lifetime = single workflow | Sessions must survive server restart |
296	| Regex-first guardrails (not LLM-as-judge) | Deterministic, fast, testable; regex covers R1–R5 exactly | Rules become too nuanced for regex (e.g. "detect hardcoded secret") |
297	| Single-brand (efood) | Spec asks for one brand; multi-brand is a path, not a feature | Second brand onboards |
298	| No auth | Spec does not specify auth; adding it would pad scope, not signal | Service goes to staging |
299	| Direct Anthropic SDK (no LangChain) | Framework would obscure the prompt/response contract, making guardrails harder to test | Team standardizes on a shared LLM gateway |
300	| `trace_id` on every entity | Free — uuid4 at creation time; enables OTEL export without schema change | OAM integration is scoped |
301	
302	## 5. If More Time
303	
304	- **OTEL export** → push `trace_id` spans to DH OAM (Observability & Monitoring); ties into PR/engineer KPI dashboard
305	- **Multi-brand AGENTS.md override** → parametrize loader by `brand`; each brand maintains its own manifesto
306	- **SKILL.md Skills pattern** (Anthropic open standard) → per-brand capability packages loadable just-in-time
307	- **SQLite persistence** → swap in-memory dict for SQLite; no schema change required (Pydantic models already serialize cleanly)
308	- **LLM-as-judge for style rules** → R3 (docstrings) is hard to catch with regex; second-pass LLM eval for WARN-tier rules
309	- **Evaluator-Optimizer loop** → let LLM re-propose patch after BLOCK guardrail fails, up to N retries

341	
342	### 1.2 Pydantic Schemas — data shape only, zero logic
343	
344	Three kinds of models. Do not confuse them.
345	
346	| Kind | Purpose | Rule |
347	|---|---|---|
348	| **LLM input schema** | What we ask the LLM to generate | Only fields the LLM creates. No id, no timestamps, no child lists. |
349	| **Response schema (Out)** | What each endpoint returns to the caller | Only fields that endpoint created. No child lists belonging to later endpoints. |
350	| **Domain model** | Storage + `GET /sessions/{id}` full state | Nested children: steps → patches → checks. Never use as LLM input or single-step response. |
351	
352	**Never pass a domain model schema to the LLM.** Child lists (`patches`, `checks`) will be filled in by the LLM, silently collapsing separate endpoints into one — violating SRP and Workflow-first.
353	
354	**Never return a domain model directly from a mutating endpoint.** Returning `PlanStep` (which includes `patches: []`) from `POST /plan` falsely implies the plan step owns patching — a structural lie in the API contract.
355	
356	File: `src/models.py`
357	
358	```python
359	from uuid import uuid4, UUID
360	from datetime import datetime, timezone
361	from typing import Literal
362	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
363	
364	Brand = Literal["efood", "glovo", "talabat"]
365	Severity = Literal["BLOCK", "WARN", "INFO"]
366	CheckResult = Literal["pass", "fail"]
367	
368	
369	# ── LLM Input Schemas ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
370	
371	class PlanStepInput(BaseModel):
372	    description: str
373	    target_files: list[str]
374	
375	class PatchProposalInput(BaseModel):
376	    diff: str
377	
378	
379	# ── Response Schemas (Out) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
380	
381	class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):
382	    """POST /plan response — no patches (Response Shape = Workflow Signal)."""
383	    id: UUID
384	    description: str
385	    target_files: list[str]
386	
387	class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):
388	    """POST /patches response — no checks (SRP: patch ≠ check)."""
389	    id: UUID
390	    planStepId: UUID
391	    diff: str
392	    created_at: datetime
393	
394	
395	# ── Domain Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
396	
397	class GuardrailCheck(BaseModel):
398	    ruleId: str
399	    severity: Severity
400	    result: CheckResult
401	    reason: str                     # must cite specific rule: "per AGENTS.md R4"
402	
403	class PatchProposal(BaseModel):
404	    id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)
405	    planStepId: UUID
406	    diff: str
407	    checks: list[GuardrailCheck] = []
408	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
409	
410	class PlanStep(BaseModel):
411	    id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)
412	    description: str
413	    target_files: list[str]
414	    patches: list[PatchProposal] = []
415	
416	class Session(BaseModel):
417	    id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)
418	    title: str
419	    description: str
420	    brand: Brand
421	    trace_id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)   # OTEL hook
422	    steps: list[PlanStep] = []
423	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
424	```
425	
426	**Success gate**: `uv run python -c "from src.models import Session, PlanStep, PatchProposal, GuardrailCheck, PlanStepInput, PatchProposalInput, PlanStepOut, PatchProposalOut"` exits 0.
427	
428	**Design principle check**:
429	- `PlanStepInput` / `PatchProposalInput` exist and are separate from domain models (SRP) ✓
430	- `PlanStepOut` / `PatchProposalOut` exist and omit child lists (Response Shape = Workflow Signal) ✓
431	- `brand: Brand` — multi-brand (JD signal) ✓
432	- `trace_id` — OTEL hook (JD signal) ✓
433	- `reason: str` — explainability: cites AGENTS.md rule ✓

571	```
572	You are implementing the deterministic guardrail layer for a DH Agentic IDE session backend.
573	Branch: feat/guardrails-deterministic. Worktree: ../wt-guardrails.

575	
576	JD context:
577	The guardrail is what makes AI-generated code "safe to deploy."
578	It implements the Engineering Manifesto (AGENTS.md) as code-enforced policy.
579	Every rule must produce a GuardrailCheck with ruleId, severity, result, and reason.
580	
581	File: src/guardrails.py
582	
583	  def check_patch(patch_text: str, brand: str) -> list[GuardrailCheck]:
584	      """Evaluate a unified diff against the brand's AGENTS.md rules.
585	      Returns one GuardrailCheck per rule, regardless of pass/fail.
586	      brand parameter is present for future multi-brand AGENTS.md routing.
587	      """
588	
589	Rules (regex-based, deterministic — no LLM calls in this file):
590	
591	  R1 — Absolute imports only
592	    Pattern: line in diff starting with +, containing "from ." (relative import)
593	    Severity: WARN
594	    Reason template: "Relative import detected: '{match}' — use absolute imports per AGENTS.md R1"
595	
596	  R2 — No os.system or subprocess without review
597	    Pattern: + line containing "os.system(" or "subprocess."
598	    Severity: BLOCK
599	    Reason template: "Unsafe shell call '{match}' — requires explicit security review per AGENTS.md R2"
600	
601	  R3 — Public functions must have docstrings
602	    Pattern: + line matching `def [a-z][^_]` (public, non-dunder) not followed by a + line with """
603	    Severity: WARN
604	    Reason: "Public function missing docstring per AGENTS.md R3"
605	
606	  R4 — No print() — use efood.logging
607	    Pattern: + line containing "print("
608	    Severity: BLOCK
609	    Reason template: "print() call detected — use efood.logging per AGENTS.md R4"
610	
611	  R5 — External HTTP via efood.http_client only
612	    Pattern: + line containing "requests." (get/post/put/delete/patch)
613	    Severity: BLOCK
614	    Reason template: "Direct requests.{method} call — use efood.http_client per AGENTS.md R5"
615	
616	Return one GuardrailCheck per rule, always. If no violation found: result="pass".
617	
618	File: tests/test_guardrails.py
619	
620	  SAMPLE_PATCH (use this for multi-rule tests):
621	    +from .utils import calc
622	    +def apply_discount(order, pct):
623	    +    print(f"Discount applied: {pct}")
624	    +    requests.get(provider_url)
625	
626	  test_r4_print_block: SAMPLE_PATCH → R4 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
627	  test_r5_requests_block: SAMPLE_PATCH → R5 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
628	  test_r1_relative_import_warn: SAMPLE_PATCH → R1 result="fail", severity="WARN"
629	  test_clean_patch_all_pass: clean patch (no violations) → all result="pass"
630	  test_r2_subprocess_block: patch with "subprocess.run(" → R2 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
631	  test_brand_parameter_accepted: check_patch("", brand="glovo") does not raise
632	
633	Done when: uv run pytest tests/test_guardrails.py -v shows 6 tests passing.
634	```

790	## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
791	
792	1. **Framework-first** — No LangChain, no LlamaIndex. Direct `anthropic` SDK only.
793	2. **Context dump** — Do NOT load AGENTS.md into a module-level variable at startup. Just-in-time.
794	3. **LLM for guardrails** — R1–R5 are regex. LLM-as-judge is P2, named as such in README.
795	4. **print() anywhere** — Use `logging`. This is also a test case.
796	5. **README last** — Skeleton (all 5 sections + Architecture diagram) before first route.
797	6. **E2E skip** — Pytest green ≠ feature correct. Always run the curl workflow.
798	7. **Missing brand on guardrail** — `check_patch(diff, brand)` signature must accept brand even if unused now.
799	8. **No trace_id** — It is one field, costs nothing, and signals OTEL/OAM awareness from the start.
800	9. **"multi-brand" only in schema** — It must also appear in README as a described extension path.
801	10. **Worktrees not visible in git log** — Merge commits should show feat/routes and feat/guardrails branch names.

# Assignment Execution Protocol
# Agentic IDE IC1 Backend Engineer — Take-Home Playbook

> **How to use**: Give me this file + the assignment file at the start of a session.
> I will execute the phases below in order, using worktrees and parallel agents where specified.
> Target: 90%+ pass-rate output within the stated time budget.

---

## JD Signal Map — Read Before Any Code

Every implementation choice below maps to a JD keyword. If a choice is not on this map, it is P2.

| JD Keyword | What to show in code | Where it lands |
|---|---|---|
| `Git worktrees` | Two worktrees, parallel branches, explicit merge step | Phase 3 setup, commit history |
| `multi-agent` | Agent A (routes) + Agent B (guardrails) on separate worktrees | Phase 3 prompt framing |
| `AGENTS.md / Engineering Manifesto` | File loaded just-in-time in every LLM call; brand-specific override path described | `src/llm.py`, README §Architecture |
| `guardrails — safe to deploy` | `GuardrailCheck(ruleId, severity, result, reason)` — BLOCK stops merge | `src/guardrails.py`, README §Domain Model |
| `multi-brand (efood/glovo/talabat)` | `brand` field on Session; guardrail loader parametrized by brand | Schema, guardrail signature |
| `context integration` | AGENTS.md injected as system-prompt context, not hardcoded strings | `src/llm.py` system prompt |
| `OTEL / OAM / tracing` | `trace_id` on Session; P2 note for OAM export | Schema + README §If More Time |
| `deterministic boundary` | Regex-first guardrails; LLM only for plan/patch generation | README §Architecture diagram |
| `developer productivity / friction` | README §Problem opens with the manual workflow this replaces | README §1 Problem |
| `customizing cutting-edge agentic IDE` | "This is not a Cursor clone — it is the DH-aware integration layer above Cursor" | README §1 first line |
| `measurement / KPI` | `GuardrailCheck` output feeds PR/engineer and Human/AI code ratio dashboards | README §If More Time |

**Mandatory before submitting**: every row above must map to ≥1 line of code or README text.

---

## Design Principles — Apply Before Writing Any Schema or Route

These five principles gate every implementation decision. If a choice conflicts with one, stop and reconsider.

### 1. SRP — Single Responsibility per Endpoint

Each endpoint = one state transition = one responsibility.

| Endpoint | Responsibility | What it must NOT do |
|---|---|---|
| `POST /sessions` | Create session entity | Generate plan, touch LLM |
| `POST /sessions/{id}/plan` | Decompose description → `[PlanStep]` with `patches: []` | Generate diffs, run guardrails |
| `POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches` | Generate diff for one PlanStep → `PatchProposal` with `checks: []` | Run guardrails, modify other steps |
| `POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check` | Run guardrails → `[GuardrailCheck]` | Generate new diffs, re-plan |
| `GET /sessions/{id}` | Return accumulated state | Trigger any computation |

**Smell check**: if a route calls both `generate()` and `check_patch()`, it violates SRP.

**Schema SRP corollary**: domain models (storage/response) and LLM input schemas are different things.
- Domain model: `PlanStep(id, description, target_files, patches=[])` — full state with children
- LLM input schema: `PlanStepInput(description, target_files)` — only what the LLM generates
- Never pass a domain model schema to the LLM if it contains nested children (id, created_at, child lists).
  The LLM will fill them in, breaking the SRP of every downstream endpoint.

---

### 2. Deterministic Boundary

The LLM is always sandwiched between deterministic code. Non-determinism is quarantined.

```
[deterministic in]  →  [LLM]  →  [deterministic out]
  schema validation     proposes    schema validation
  AGENTS.md load        only        guardrail regex
  brand resolution               Pydantic parsing
```

Rules:
- `src/guardrails.py` contains **zero** LLM calls. Regex only.
- `src/llm.py` contains **zero** business logic. It is a transport layer.
- LLM output is always parsed through Pydantic before it touches application state.
- Use `tool_use` + `tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}` — never free-text JSON parsing.

---

### 3. YAGNI — You Aren't Gonna Need It

Build exactly what the spec asks. Nothing more.

| Temptation | Decision |
|---|---|
| SQLite / Postgres | In-memory dict. Persistence is P2 — document the swap path. |
| Auth / API keys | Not in spec. Padding scope is a red flag for evaluators. |
| LLM-as-judge for guardrails | Regex covers R1–R5 exactly. LLM-as-judge is P2. |
| Retry loop / Evaluator-Optimizer | P2. Document it in README §If More Time. |
| Multi-brand AGENTS.md routing | `brand` parameter in signature. Second brand file is P2. |
| Caching | Not needed within a 90-min session lifetime. |

If it is not in the spec and not a JD signal, it is P2 — mention in README, do not implement.

**YAGNI applies to response shape too — Response Schema = Workflow Signal.**

The shape of a response communicates what the endpoint did. Empty child collections in a response are not "free" — they send a false signal.

```
# Wrong: POST /plan returns this
{"id": "...", "description": "...", "target_files": [...], "patches": []}
#                                                           ^^^^^^^^^^
#                                              implies patches are part of planning

# Right: POST /plan returns this
{"id": "...", "description": "...", "target_files": [...]}
# shape says: "I produced a plan step. patches are someone else's job."
```

Rule: **each endpoint's response schema contains only the fields that endpoint created.**
Use a dedicated response model (e.g. `PlanStepOut`, `PatchProposalOut`) without child lists — do not reuse the full domain model as the response type.

```python
# models.py — response schemas (one per endpoint that creates a new entity)
class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]
    # no patches — POST /plan does not create patches

class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    planStepId: UUID
    diff: str
    # no checks — POST /patches does not run guardrails
```

The full domain model (`PlanStep` with `patches`, `PatchProposal` with `checks`) is only used in `GET /sessions/{id}` — the accumulated state view.

---

### 4. Workflow-first Design

Design the workflow (sequence of state transitions) before designing schemas or routes.

**Step 1 — Draw the workflow as a state machine first:**
```
∅ → Session(steps=[]) → Session(steps=[PlanStep(patches=[])]) → Session(steps=[PlanStep(patches=[PatchProposal(checks=[])])]) → Session(steps=[...(checks=[GuardrailCheck...])])
```

**Step 2 — Each arrow = one endpoint.**
Each endpoint advances state by exactly one step. No endpoint skips a step or does two steps at once.

**Step 3 — Schema flows from workflow, not from database convenience.**
Ask: "what is the minimal input the LLM needs to do its job?" → that is the LLM input schema.
Ask: "what is the full state a reviewing engineer needs?" → that is the domain model.

---

### 5. Explainability

Every AI decision must be traceable by a human reviewer.

| Layer | Explainability mechanism |
|---|---|
| Plan generation | `PlanStep.description` is human-readable; `target_files` is explicit |
| Patch generation | `PatchProposal.diff` is a standard unified diff — reviewable in any diff tool |
| Guardrail result | `GuardrailCheck.ruleId` + `reason` — cites the specific AGENTS.md rule |
| Session audit | `Session.trace_id` — future OTEL/OAM hook |
| AI vs human work | README §AI Leverage table — explicit, honest accounting |

**Rule**: No GuardrailCheck result without a `reason` that names the specific rule (`per AGENTS.md R4`).
A `result: "fail"` with `reason: "error"` is worse than useless — it is unactionable.

---

## Meta-Principles (from Anthropic engineering blog + agent-architecture.md)

- **Deterministic sandwich**: Every LLM call sits between deterministic input validation and deterministic output verification. The LLM proposes; the code decides.
- **Workflow backbone + one agent loop**: LLM loop only where genuinely non-deterministic. Guardrails are always deterministic.
- **Just-in-time context**: Load AGENTS.md at call time — never pre-dump into module-level startup.
- **Single agent > multi-agent for the coding loop**: Use parallel worktrees for *independent implementation tracks*, not for agents collaborating on the same task.
- **README is the deliverable**: Code is proof it works. The README demonstrates you think like a system designer.
- **DH framing**: This service is a "DH-aware integration layer" — it augments Cursor/Claude Code with brand context and guardrails. It is not a replacement IDE.

## Slash Command Contract — `/assignment`

The project command lives in both `.claude/commands/assignment.md` and
`case-01-session-backend/.claude/commands/assignment.md`. Keep them in sync.

Pattern borrowed from Anthropic's `anthropics/claude-code` project commands:

- Use command frontmatter (`description`, `allowed-tools`) so the command advertises its purpose and narrows tool use.
- Inject live context at invocation time (`git status`, assignment text, protocol, AGENTS.md) instead of relying on stale memory.
- Treat `$ARGUMENTS` as extra user input, not as a replacement for the assignment or protocol.
- Start with a todo list and update it as phases complete.
- Never invent requirements. If the assignment is ambiguous, document the assumption rather than expanding scope.
- Be conservative with automation: do not push, open PRs, or post external comments unless the user explicitly asks.
- End every `/assignment` run with files changed, verification run, and remaining checks.

If a command behavior conflicts with this protocol, the protocol wins.

---

## Phase 0 — Decompose (0:00 – 0:05)

**Who runs this**: Me (Claude Code), immediately on receiving the assignment.

### 0.1 XY-30S Decomposition (4 bullets, 30 seconds)

```
- WHO uses this?           → developer requesting an AI-assisted code change
- WHAT friction removed?   → manual diff review + rule compliance check against AGENTS.md
- NON-DETERMINISTIC parts? → plan decomposition, patch generation (LLM); rule checking (regex)
- EXTERNAL integrations?   → Anthropic API, AGENTS.md file, brand config
```

### 0.2 Risk Inventory

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| LLM latency in tests | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` missing → mock mode; real call behind flag |
| Storage underspecified | In-memory dict; documented swap path to Postgres |
| Rule ambiguity in AGENTS.md | Regex-first; LLM-as-judge only if regex is insufficient |
| Multi-brand complexity | `brand` field drives AGENTS.md path; single override dict per brand |
| Time overrun | P0/P1/P2 locked before first line of code |

### 0.3 P0 / P1 / P2 Cut List

| Tier | Scope | Time |
|------|-------|------|
| **P0** | All 5 endpoints + 2 deterministic guardrails (R4, R5 minimum) + 1 pytest green | 0–75 min |
| **P1** | Real Anthropic SDK call with structured JSON output; `trace_id` on every response; brand-parametrized guardrail loader | 75–85 min |
| **P2** | OTEL export to OAM; SQLite persistence; multi-brand AGENTS.md override; auth; SKILL.md Skills pattern | README mention only |

---

## Phase 1 — README Skeleton + Domain Model (0:05 – 0:20)

### 1.1 README.md — Write the 5 sections in English, all blanks filled with placeholders

```markdown
# Session Backend — Agentic IDE Integration Layer

> This service is the DH-aware integration layer that sits above Cursor/Claude Code:
> it injects brand context (AGENTS.md), proposes code patches via LLM, and enforces
> guardrails before any generated diff can be merged.
> The LLM proposes; deterministic checks decide.

## 1. Problem & Approach

**What this replaces**: a developer manually reviewing LLM-generated diffs against
their brand's Engineering Manifesto (AGENTS.md) before merging.

**Architecture**: deterministic sandwich —
- deterministic input (AGENTS.md loaded just-in-time, brand resolved from session)
- LLM call (plan decomposition or patch generation, schema-constrained output)
- deterministic output (guardrail regex checks, severity-gated merge decision)

**Assumptions** (5+):
1. In-memory storage is sufficient for a session-scoped prototype; persistence is P2.
2. AGENTS.md lives at the repo root and is the authoritative rule source.
3. Severity BLOCK = merge blocked; WARN = visible to developer but not blocking.
4. LLM output is always validated against the Pydantic schema before storage.
5. Each brand (efood/glovo/talabat) can have its own AGENTS.md path; currently efood only.
6. `trace_id` is generated per-session for future OTEL export.

**Ambiguities I noticed**:
1. Should WARN rules accumulate across patches and block after N warnings?
2. Is the LLM allowed to propose changes to AGENTS.md itself?
3. Who owns rule addition — platform team or brand team?

## 2. Domain Model

- `Session` — top-level entity; carries `brand`, `trace_id`, and all child lists
- `PlanStep` — one proposed change (description + target_files)
- `PatchProposal` — unified diff string for one PlanStep
- `GuardrailCheck` — one rule result: `ruleId`, `severity`, `result`, `reason`

```
Session 1—* PlanStep 1—* PatchProposal 1—* GuardrailCheck
```

Severity ladder: `BLOCK` (security/brand violation) > `WARN` (style) > `INFO` (suggestion).
A patch with any `BLOCK` check cannot be merged. This feeds the Human/AI code ratio KPI
by flagging which AI-generated diffs required human intervention.

## 3. AI Leverage

| Part | Done by | Verification |
|------|---------|--------------|
| Domain models | Hand | Type-checked by Pydantic + mypy |
| FastAPI routes | AI (Claude Code, worktree feat/routes) | Hand-reviewed diff |
| Guardrail regex rules | AI (Claude Code, worktree feat/guardrails) | Pytest: R2/R4/R5 BLOCK cases |
| LLM prompt design | Hand | Manual smoke test with real API key |
| README | Hand | — |

Every AI-generated file went through: `ruff check` → `pytest` → manual diff scan.
No AI output was committed without a test covering the specific behavior.

"The LLM only performs extraction and proposal generation.
Merging, validation, and guardrail evaluation are deterministic."

## 4. Trade-offs & Decisions

| Decision | Rationale | Reconsider if |
|----------|-----------|---------------|
| In-memory storage | Removes DB setup from the critical path; session lifetime = single workflow | Sessions must survive server restart |
| Regex-first guardrails (not LLM-as-judge) | Deterministic, fast, testable; regex covers R1–R5 exactly | Rules become too nuanced for regex (e.g. "detect hardcoded secret") |
| Single-brand (efood) | Spec asks for one brand; multi-brand is a path, not a feature | Second brand onboards |
| No auth | Spec does not specify auth; adding it would pad scope, not signal | Service goes to staging |
| Direct Anthropic SDK (no LangChain) | Framework would obscure the prompt/response contract, making guardrails harder to test | Team standardizes on a shared LLM gateway |
| `trace_id` on every entity | Free — uuid4 at creation time; enables OTEL export without schema change | OAM integration is scoped |

## 5. If More Time

- **OTEL export** → push `trace_id` spans to DH OAM (Observability & Monitoring); ties into PR/engineer KPI dashboard
- **Multi-brand AGENTS.md override** → parametrize loader by `brand`; each brand maintains its own manifesto
- **SKILL.md Skills pattern** (Anthropic open standard) → per-brand capability packages loadable just-in-time
- **SQLite persistence** → swap in-memory dict for SQLite; no schema change required (Pydantic models already serialize cleanly)
- **LLM-as-judge for style rules** → R3 (docstrings) is hard to catch with regex; second-pass LLM eval for WARN-tier rules
- **Evaluator-Optimizer loop** → let LLM re-propose patch after BLOCK guardrail fails, up to N retries

## How to Run

```bash
uv sync
cp .env.example .env     # set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (or leave blank for mock mode)
uv run pytest            # all tests green
uv run uvicorn src.main:app --reload
# → http://localhost:8000/docs
```

Full workflow:
```bash
# 1. create session
SESSION=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"add discount","description":"add pricing/discount.py","brand":"efood"}' \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")

# 2. plan
curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION/plan | python3 -m json.tool

# 3. patch (use planStepId from step 2)
PATCH=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION/patches \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"planStepId":"<id>"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")

# 4. check — expect R4 and R5 to flag BLOCK
curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION/patches/$PATCH/check | python3 -m json.tool
```
```

### 1.2 Pydantic Schemas — data shape only, zero logic

Three kinds of models. Do not confuse them.

| Kind | Purpose | Rule |
|---|---|---|
| **LLM input schema** | What we ask the LLM to generate | Only fields the LLM creates. No id, no timestamps, no child lists. |
| **Response schema (Out)** | What each endpoint returns to the caller | Only fields that endpoint created. No child lists belonging to later endpoints. |
| **Domain model** | Storage + `GET /sessions/{id}` full state | Nested children: steps → patches → checks. Never use as LLM input or single-step response. |

**Never pass a domain model schema to the LLM.** Child lists (`patches`, `checks`) will be filled in by the LLM, silently collapsing separate endpoints into one — violating SRP and Workflow-first.

**Never return a domain model directly from a mutating endpoint.** Returning `PlanStep` (which includes `patches: []`) from `POST /plan` falsely implies the plan step owns patching — a structural lie in the API contract.

File: `src/models.py`

```python
from uuid import uuid4, UUID
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Literal
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

Brand = Literal["efood", "glovo", "talabat"]
Severity = Literal["BLOCK", "WARN", "INFO"]
CheckResult = Literal["pass", "fail"]


# ── LLM Input Schemas ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class PlanStepInput(BaseModel):
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]

class PatchProposalInput(BaseModel):
    diff: str


# ── Response Schemas (Out) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):
    """POST /plan response — no patches (Response Shape = Workflow Signal)."""
    id: UUID
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]

class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):
    """POST /patches response — no checks (SRP: patch ≠ check)."""
    id: UUID
    planStepId: UUID
    diff: str
    created_at: datetime


# ── Domain Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class GuardrailCheck(BaseModel):
    ruleId: str
    severity: Severity
    result: CheckResult
    reason: str                     # must cite specific rule: "per AGENTS.md R4"

class PatchProposal(BaseModel):
    id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)
    planStepId: UUID
    diff: str
    checks: list[GuardrailCheck] = []
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

class PlanStep(BaseModel):
    id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)
    description: str
    target_files: list[str]
    patches: list[PatchProposal] = []

class Session(BaseModel):
    id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)
    title: str
    description: str
    brand: Brand
    trace_id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)   # OTEL hook
    steps: list[PlanStep] = []
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
```

**Success gate**: `uv run python -c "from src.models import Session, PlanStep, PatchProposal, GuardrailCheck, PlanStepInput, PatchProposalInput, PlanStepOut, PatchProposalOut"` exits 0.

**Design principle check**:
- `PlanStepInput` / `PatchProposalInput` exist and are separate from domain models (SRP) ✓
- `PlanStepOut` / `PatchProposalOut` exist and omit child lists (Response Shape = Workflow Signal) ✓
- `brand: Brand` — multi-brand (JD signal) ✓
- `trace_id` — OTEL hook (JD signal) ✓
- `reason: str` — explainability: cites AGENTS.md rule ✓

---

## Phase 2 — Architecture Diagram in README (0:20 – 0:25)

Add this to README §1 before writing any route. This diagram is what the evaluator reads in 30 seconds to assess system thinking.

```
Architecture — Deterministic Sandwich:

Developer Request
        │
        ▼
[POST /sessions]
  brand: "efood" → resolves AGENTS.md path         ← deterministic

[POST /sessions/{id}/plan]
  load AGENTS.md just-in-time                       ← deterministic
  → LLM(claude-sonnet-4-6): decompose → [PlanStep] ← non-deterministic, schema-constrained
  → validate: target_files non-empty               ← deterministic

[POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches]
  → LLM: generate unified diff for PlanStep        ← non-deterministic
  → store as PatchProposal(diff=...)

[POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check]
  → load AGENTS.md for brand just-in-time          ← deterministic
  → for each rule: regex check → GuardrailCheck    ← deterministic
  → any BLOCK? → merge gated                       ← deterministic

[GET /sessions/{id}]
  → full state: steps + patches + checks + trace_id
```

**Opening sentence** (copy verbatim into README §1):
> "This service converts implicit human debugging workflows into explicit, observable agent workflows — the LLM proposes, deterministic checks decide."

**Positioning sentence** (copy verbatim, 2nd line of README intro):
> "It is not a Cursor clone — it is the DH-aware integration layer that injects brand context (AGENTS.md) and enforces Engineering Manifesto guardrails before any AI-generated patch can be merged."

---

## Phase 3 — Implementation (0:25 – 1:15)

### Worktree Setup — Explicit JD Signal

The worktree split is **not just an efficiency choice** — it demonstrates the JD requirement: "Git worktrees and modern development workflows." Name the branches so the git log tells the story.

```bash
# From repo root (main branch already has models.py)
git worktree add ../wt-routes -b feat/routes-llm-integration
git worktree add ../wt-guardrails -b feat/guardrails-deterministic
```

Both agents run concurrently. Each sees the same `src/models.py` (committed to main before Phase 3).

---

### Agent A Prompt — Routes + LLM Integration (worktree: wt-routes)

```
You are implementing the FastAPI routes for a DH Agentic IDE session backend.
Branch: feat/routes-llm-integration. Worktree: ../wt-routes.
src/models.py is already committed on main — do NOT modify it.

Positioning context (important for README alignment):
This service is the "DH-aware integration layer" above Cursor/Claude Code.
It injects AGENTS.md (the brand's Engineering Manifesto) as just-in-time context
into every LLM call — not as a pre-loaded module variable.

Files to create:
  src/llm.py    — LLM client wrapper
  src/routes.py — 5 FastAPI route handlers
  src/main.py   — app factory + router registration + /health

1. src/llm.py
   - Two functions: generate(prompt, brand, schema) -> dict  and  generate_list(prompt, brand, schema) -> list[dict]
   - Loads AGENTS.md for the brand just-in-time: Path(f"{brand}/AGENTS.md").read_text()
   - Uses tool_use for structured output — NEVER free-text JSON parsing:
       tools=[{"name":"output","description":"...","input_schema": schema.model_json_schema()}]
       tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}
       result = response.content[0].input   # already a dict, no json.loads needed
   - max_tokens=4096 minimum
   - generate_list wraps schema in: {"type":"object","properties":{"items":{"type":"array","items":schema.model_json_schema()}},"required":["items"]}
     and returns result["items"]
   - If ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set: return deterministic mock (do not crash)
   - CRITICAL: routes call generate(prompt, brand, PlanStepInput) and generate(prompt, brand, PatchProposalInput)
     — NEVER generate(prompt, brand, PlanStep) or generate(prompt, brand, PatchProposal)
     — Domain models contain child lists; passing them to LLM violates SRP
   - Functions ≤ 30 lines. No print(). Use logging.

2. src/routes.py — all routes use sessions: dict[str, Session] from src/store.py

   Use three-tier schemas — NEVER return a domain model from a mutating endpoint:
   - LLM calls: generate(prompt, brand, PlanStepInput), generate(prompt, brand, PatchProposalInput)
   - POST /plan response_model=list[PlanStepOut] — return PlanStepOut per step (no patches field)
   - POST /patches response_model=PatchProposalOut — return PatchProposalOut (no checks field)
   - GET /sessions/{id} response_model=Session — only endpoint that returns full nested state

   POST /sessions
     body: {title, description, brand}
     → Session(title=..., description=..., brand=...)
     → store in dict, return session

   POST /sessions/{id}/plan
     → call generate_list(prompt, brand, PlanStepInput) → list[dict]
     → steps = [PlanStep(**s) for s in raw_steps]; attach to session.steps
     → return [PlanStepOut(id=s.id, description=s.description, target_files=s.target_files) for s in steps]

   POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches
     step_id is a path param — no request body needed
     → call generate(prompt, brand, PatchProposalInput) → dict
     → patch = PatchProposal(**raw, planStepId=step.id); attach to step.patches
     → return PatchProposalOut(id=patch.id, planStepId=patch.planStepId, diff=patch.diff, created_at=patch.created_at)

   POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check
     → import check_patch from src.guardrails (stub: raise ImportError gracefully if missing)
     → attach results to patch.checks, return list[GuardrailCheck]

   GET /sessions/{id}
     → return full Session (all nested lists included)

3. src/store.py
   sessions: dict[str, Session] = {}

Constraints:
- All imports absolute (from src.models import ...)
- ruff check must pass
- No print() anywhere

Done when: uvicorn src.main:app starts and GET /health returns {"status": "ok"}.
```

---

### Agent B Prompt — Guardrails + Tests (worktree: wt-guardrails)

```
You are implementing the deterministic guardrail layer for a DH Agentic IDE session backend.
Branch: feat/guardrails-deterministic. Worktree: ../wt-guardrails.
src/models.py is already committed on main — do NOT modify it.

JD context:
The guardrail is what makes AI-generated code "safe to deploy."
It implements the Engineering Manifesto (AGENTS.md) as code-enforced policy.
Every rule must produce a GuardrailCheck with ruleId, severity, result, and reason.

File: src/guardrails.py

  def check_patch(patch_text: str, brand: str) -> list[GuardrailCheck]:
      """Evaluate a unified diff against the brand's AGENTS.md rules.
      Returns one GuardrailCheck per rule, regardless of pass/fail.
      brand parameter is present for future multi-brand AGENTS.md routing.
      """

Rules (regex-based, deterministic — no LLM calls in this file):

  R1 — Absolute imports only
    Pattern: line in diff starting with +, containing "from ." (relative import)
    Severity: WARN
    Reason template: "Relative import detected: '{match}' — use absolute imports per AGENTS.md R1"

  R2 — No os.system or subprocess without review
    Pattern: + line containing "os.system(" or "subprocess."
    Severity: BLOCK
    Reason template: "Unsafe shell call '{match}' — requires explicit security review per AGENTS.md R2"

  R3 — Public functions must have docstrings
    Pattern: + line matching `def [a-z][^_]` (public, non-dunder) not followed by a + line with """
    Severity: WARN
    Reason: "Public function missing docstring per AGENTS.md R3"

  R4 — No print() — use efood.logging
    Pattern: + line containing "print("
    Severity: BLOCK
    Reason template: "print() call detected — use efood.logging per AGENTS.md R4"

  R5 — External HTTP via efood.http_client only
    Pattern: + line containing "requests." (get/post/put/delete/patch)
    Severity: BLOCK
    Reason template: "Direct requests.{method} call — use efood.http_client per AGENTS.md R5"

Return one GuardrailCheck per rule, always. If no violation found: result="pass".

File: tests/test_guardrails.py

  SAMPLE_PATCH (use this for multi-rule tests):
    +from .utils import calc
    +def apply_discount(order, pct):
    +    print(f"Discount applied: {pct}")
    +    requests.get(provider_url)

  test_r4_print_block: SAMPLE_PATCH → R4 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
  test_r5_requests_block: SAMPLE_PATCH → R5 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
  test_r1_relative_import_warn: SAMPLE_PATCH → R1 result="fail", severity="WARN"
  test_clean_patch_all_pass: clean patch (no violations) → all result="pass"
  test_r2_subprocess_block: patch with "subprocess.run(" → R2 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
  test_brand_parameter_accepted: check_patch("", brand="glovo") does not raise

Done when: uv run pytest tests/test_guardrails.py -v shows 6 tests passing.
```

---

### Merge + Integration (after both agents complete)

```bash
cd /path/to/main-repo
git merge feat/routes-llm-integration
git merge feat/guardrails-deterministic

# Wire guardrail into /check route:
# src/routes.py: replace `return []` stub with:
#   from src.guardrails import check_patch
#   results = check_patch(patch.diff, session.brand)
```

Verify integration:
```bash
uv run pytest                        # all tests green
uv run uvicorn src.main:app --reload # server starts
```

---

## Phase 4 — E2E Validation (1:15 – 1:25)

Unit tests verify code correctness. This phase verifies **feature correctness** — the evaluator's actual test.

```bash
# 1. create efood session
SESSION=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"discount module","description":"add pricing/discount.py with apply_discount","brand":"efood"}' \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['id'])")

echo "Session: $SESSION"

# 2. plan
STEP=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION/plan \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d[0]['id'])")

echo "Step: $STEP"

# 3. patch
PATCH=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION/steps/$STEP/patches \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")

echo "Patch: $PATCH"

# 4. check — MUST see R4 and R5 as BLOCK
curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION/steps/$STEP/patches/$PATCH/check \
  | python3 -m json.tool

# 5. full state
curl -s localhost:8000/sessions/$SESSION | python3 -m json.tool
```

**Pass criteria**:
- R4 (`print`) → `severity: "BLOCK"`, `result: "fail"` ✓
- R5 (`requests`) → `severity: "BLOCK"`, `result: "fail"` ✓
- `trace_id` present on Session ✓
- Full state returns nested steps → patches → checks ✓

---

## Phase 5 — README Finalize + JD Alignment (1:25 – 1:30)

Fill every blank in the skeleton. Then run the JD alignment check below.

### JD Alignment Check (must all be green before submit)

| JD Requirement | Evidence in repo | ✓ |
|---|---|---|
| Git worktrees | `git log --oneline --graph` shows feat/routes + feat/guardrails merged | ☐ |
| Multi-agent workflow | README §3 AI Leverage table shows Agent A / Agent B with separate worktrees | ☐ |
| AGENTS.md / Engineering Manifesto | README §Architecture: "loaded just-in-time"; code: `src/llm.py` reads file at call time | ☐ |
| Guardrails — safe to deploy | `GuardrailCheck.severity=BLOCK` gates merge; R4 + R5 demonstrated in E2E | ☐ |
| Multi-brand field | `Session.brand: Brand` in schema; `check_patch(patch, brand=session.brand)` in route | ☐ |
| OTEL / OAM / tracing | `Session.trace_id` present; README §5 names OAM export as P2 | ☐ |
| Deterministic boundary | README diagram shows LLM between two deterministic blocks; guardrails are regex-only | ☐ |
| Context integration framing | README intro: "DH-aware integration layer" not "build an IDE" | ☐ |
| Developer productivity / friction | README §1 opens with "what this replaces" — the manual workflow | ☐ |
| Measurement / KPI | README §Domain Model mentions Human/AI code ratio as downstream metric | ☐ |

### Design Principles Check (run before JD alignment)

| Principle | Verification |
|---|---|
| SRP — POST /plan response has no `patches` field | `curl .../plan \| python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert 'patches' not in d[0]"` |
| SRP — POST /patches response has no `checks` field | Same pattern: `assert 'checks' not in d` |
| SRP — LLM input schemas exist and are separate | `grep -n "PlanStepInput\|PatchProposalInput" src/models.py` shows two classes |
| SRP — Response schemas (Out) exist and are separate | `grep -n "PlanStepOut\|PatchProposalOut" src/models.py` shows two classes |
| Deterministic Boundary — no LLM call in guardrails.py | `grep -n "anthropic\|client\." src/guardrails.py` returns nothing |
| YAGNI — no unspecified features | No SQLite, no auth, no retry loop unless spec asked |
| Workflow-first — GET /sessions/{id} shows accumulated state | Full nested state only after all endpoints called in sequence |
| Explainability — reason cites rule | `grep "per AGENTS.md" src/guardrails.py` returns one line per rule |

**All 10 green = submit. Any red = fix README or add one line of code.**
